Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - College-Town Champs
Description: Guy's rolling into college towns; in Columbus, Ohio, a pierogi paradise dishing out their specialties and a barbecue truck smoking premiere pork; in Boulder, Colorado, a legit Thai joint serving knockout noodles and off-the-chain chicken.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - International Intrigue
Description: In Dallas, a funky Thai joint serves up noodles, curry and green papaya salad; in Miami, a Latin place deals in empanadas and a unique plantain sandwich; real Ethiopian favorites in Tucson, Ariz.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Lights-Out Latin
Description: In Miami, Fla., a husband-and-wife team dishes out primo Peruvian sandwiches; in Wilmington, N.C., a funky joint cooks up the flavors of Panama; in Tucson, Ariz., a funky fresh spin on tacos and carne asada.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Mega Meat and Sweet Treats
Description: Guy Fieri dives into mega meat and a little bit of sweet in Atlanta, Austin, Texas, and Richland, Wash.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Crazy Chicken and Twisted Burgers
Description: Guy Fieri's digging into crazy twists on chicken and burgers; in Wilmington, Del., there's a bourbon bar that's piling pork onto a patty and doctoring up chicken skins.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Bagels, Biscuit and Boar
Description: Guy Fieri is diggin' into all kinds of flavor, starting in Santa Fe, N.M., at a pub serving comfort grub like chicken and biscuits and in-the-shell clam chowder.

Subject: Wildcard Kitchen - LA in the House
Description: Eric Adjepong hosts Los Angeles legends Mei Lin, Marcel Vigneron and Chris Oh at his culinary card game; the West Coast friends each have $5,000 on the line as they attempt to put the best spin on meat and potatoes to take the jackpot.

Subject: Wildcard Kitchen - Chopped High Rollers
Description: Eric Adjepong invites ``Chopped`` judges Maneet Chauhan, Brian Malarkey, and Marc Murphy to his legendary card game; they double down with $10,000 as a battle over eggs benedict determines if all three chefs have enough cash for the final hand.

Subject: Wildcard Kitchen - Guys' Night Out
Description: Eric Adjepong invites three of his favorite guys, Jet Tila,Eddie Jackson and Viet Pham, to his after-hours card game. These epic chefs each arrive with $5,000 for a night of card playing, fast-paced cooking and some major trash talking.

Subject: Wildcard Kitchen - Clash of the Restaurant Queens
Description: Chef Antonia Lofaso returns for another wild night at Eric Adjepong's high-stakes culinary poker showdown! This time, she's betting against her best buddies and acclaimed restaurateurs Ayesha Nurdjaja and Claudette Zepeda.

Subject: Guy's Grocery Games - GGG Eggs-travaganza
Description: Guy Fieri discovers Hunter and Antonia Lofaso holding an all-star competition featuring his least favorite food; the chefs try to get Guy to take bites of egg-heavy dishes for points and entice the judges with meals made from unpopular ingredients.

Subject: Guy's Grocery Games - DDD Guilty Pleasures
Description: Guy Fieri invites four chefs from ``Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives`` to cook their creations; they make their favorite guilty pleasure using no more than 6.5 pounds of ingredients and feature whammy items in their diner dinner.

Subject: Guy's Grocery Games - The GGG Experience
Description: Guy Fieri gives an unfiltered behind-the-scenes view of the Flavortown Market experience; from the chefs' arrival to the market tour and Guy's pre-show pep talk -- all will be revealed for the first time.

Subject: Guy's Grocery Games - Best Burgers
Description: Guy Fieri brings in four baller burgermeisters for a bodacious burger battle; they must make an excellent cheeseburger using 10 items or fewer before incorporating an alternate ground meat and unusual produce item in their winning burger and fries.

Subject: Guy's Grocery Games - DDD-elicious
Description: Guy Fieri invites some of the newest chefs from ``Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives`` to compete in Flavortown Market, where they make their best seller; the chefs roll the GGG dice to see what mandatory items they must use in an elevated comfort classic.

Subject: Guy's Grocery Games - Culinary Call-Outs
Description: Returning chefs call out their market rivals for a $10,000 showdown to settle the score; a Seoul food chef challenges the opponent who beat her by one point, and a five-time GGG winner calls out the judge who defeated her in a cheesy battle.

Subject: Guy's Grocery Games - Meatless Masters
Description: Guy Fieri welcomes back former GGG winners who use their meatless dishes to show the meat eaters how it's done; they make a dish without any carts before shopping each aisle to make a 10-item winner's dinner.

Subject: Guy's Grocery Games - Beat the Judges: Non-Stop Noodles
Description: Guy Fieri welcomes three chefs who won in the past to take on each other in a non-stop noodle event; the winner gets a shot at turning their prize money into $20,000 if they can beat a GGG judge in a noodle cook-off.

Subject: Guy's Grocery Games - New Food Stars
Description: Guy Fieri welcomes three up-and-coming food stars with big social media followings to take part in a viral-worthy culinary competition. The competitors must prepare their signature dish using only the ingredients they can fit in a tiny shopping bag.

Subject: Guy's Grocery Games - One Dish Redemption
Description: Four chefs who fell short of victory their first time around are back to cook a redemption dinner; Guy Fieri goes behind the scenes to reveal Flavortown secrets, including the workings of this real deal grocery store inside a sound stage.

Description: Four chefs try to not get kicked to the curb when Alex Guarnaschelli asks them to create a curbside breakfast; the chefs pilfer the parking lot looking for ingredients worthy of a poolside lunch and the finalists rummage for a romantic dinner dish.

Description: It's a battle of the South vs. Southwest as chef Charlie Loomis of Chattanooga faces off against chef Israel Rivera from Albuquerque, N.M. for a shot at Bobby Flay.

Subject: Tournament of Champions - Time for Battle
Description: Guy Fieri kicks off year five; from big names in past seasons to new chefs with the potential to go all the way, they'll all face off to win the belt, the coveted title and $150,000.

Subject: Baking Championship: Next Gen - A Trip to Remember
Description: Duff Goldman and Kardea Brown challenge the sibling baking teams to share their family trip memories by making vacation desserts; the bakers put their teamwork to the test to recreate a memorable food experience from a location they visited.

Subject: Baking Championship: Next Gen - Puzzle Piece of Cake
Description: Duff Goldman and Kardea Brown challenge the sibling teams to create two separate cakes that fit together just like a puzzle; then, the bakers must decorate each puzzle cake piece to represent.

Subject: Guy's Grocery Games - Winner's World Cup
Description: Guy invites three chefs with previous wins to go head-to-head in a showdown of international flavors; the competitors must shop in Flavortown Market's new international section, where all the labels are in different languages.

Subject: Guy's Grocery Games - Supermarket of Mischief
Description: Guy Fieri invites four superfans to Flavortown Market for a night of twisted games and shenanigans; for the first round, Guy serves up a whole grocery list of games for the chefs to play while they cook up their signature dishes.

Subject: Guy's Grocery Games - Flavortown Food Network Champs
Description: Four competition-winning chefs battle it out for the title of Flavortown Food Network Champ and a $15,000 prize; they compete in a pair of sudden-death matches and make a seafood dinner using the food pyramid.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Poutine, Pizza and Pork
Description: This trip, Guy Fieri travels the country diving into some meaty masterpieces starting at a spot in Longmont, Colorado, that serves craft beer and primo pub grub like their piled-high poutine burger.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Delicious Discoveries
Description: This trip, Guy Fieri uncovers some surprising culinary discoveries; in Columbus, Ohio, a hidden gem tucked away in a grocery store serves out-of-bounds Nepalese-Tibetan specialties.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Belly, Beef and Bratwurst
Description: In Atlanta, a funky Asian spot puts out prime-time pork belly and wok-fried chicken; in Vancouver, an elevated diner dishes out duck pancakes and not-your-momma's meatloaf; in Kennewick, Wash., a brewpub pairs pints with house-made sausages.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Coming Together in Cali
Description: Guy visits Chico, Calif., celebrating the town's food scene as residents recover from ravaging fires; a wood-fired Italian spot cooks up pork ragu; a bakery serves up savory specialties like black bean chili; a pub serves a ``Pho-rench Dip.``

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Full of Surprises
Description: In Santa Fe, N.M., a culinary pub serves lights-out lamb neck and tamales; in Kansas City, Mo., a funky tapas joint dishes out duck tongue tacos and braised pork jowl; in El Paso, Texas, a deli makes pupusas and fries from chickpeas.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Off the Beaten Path
Description: In Richmond is a jammin' Jamaican joint serving outrageous oxtail and fried chicken with true island flavor; in Reno is a sandwich spot going loco with the bird and putting a unique twist on the breakfast burrito.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Meat Sampler
Description: A carnivore haven stacking sandwiches with dynamite duck and a pork trifecta; a taco joint loading up out-of-bounds oxtail; a cider house adding their specialty drinks to their kitchen creations, like fiery apple wings and a lamb French dip.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Creative Creations
Description: On this trip, Guy Fieri is divin' into inventive eats; in Burlington, Vt., a funky joint cooking up mac and cheese pancakes; in Richmond, Va., going gangbusters with grits; artistic eatery in Ottawa, Canada.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Mouthwatering Meat
Description: Brisket and burgers at a butcher shop-restaurant in Palm Beach, Fla.; Latin-style pork belly and Caribbean oxtails in Houston, Texas.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Regional Flavors
Description: Guy Fieri digs into regional flavors where you may not expect to find them, like Cajun jambalaya in Bend, Ore.; Pakistani oxtail in Chehalis, Wash.; a tiki joint in Houston serves elevated dumplings and honey butter chicken biscuits.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Stellar Sandwiches and Big-Time Burgers
Description: Guy Fieri's unhinging his jaw for some sandwiches and big-time burgers; in Palm Desert, Calif., a market-deli is piling up the porchetta; a burger joint is adding over-the-top twists; in Nashville, Tenn., a Vietnamese spot is making the bomb banh mi.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - From Chicken to Cheesesteak
Description: Guy Fieri is digging into chicken and some crazy creations; in Palm Springs, Calif., a crepe spot is all about the savory, while a joint is cooking real deal Mexican pollo; an Egyptian food truck is serving wings and a falafel waffle.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Beef and Bird
Description: Loading up the meat with bomb beef and bird; in North Conway, N.H., a funky joint is putting Thai in their tacos, and a local gem in Emerald Isle, N.C., is firin' up big barbecue; in Taos, N.M., a traditional spot serves up New Mexican specialties.

Subject: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives - Mexican, Mediterranean and Mofongo
Description: Guy Fieri's grabbing an international playlist of flavor; in Florida, a funky spot is putting a Mediterranean spin on burgers, while a local gem is dishing out Puerto Rican specialties; in Las Vegas, a real deal Mexican joint has bomb birria tacos.

Description: The diversity of the Amazon and the strategies used by the jungle life to make the most of limited nutrients.

Subject: Africa's Wild Year - Autumn
Description: Autumn in Africa brings opportunities; cape fur seals, Samango monkeys, chameleons, impala, and more respond to the season; autumn triggers changes in the lives of many animals; rare creatures reveal themselves and predators gather to take advantage.

Subject: Africa's Wild Year - Winter
Description: Winter in Southern Africa brings drought, ice and storms; the animals that live here must compete with the weather, the land and each other; cheetahs, seals, vultures, and meerkats; rains finally end the dry season and trigger a burst of life.

Subject: Africa's Wild Year - Spring
Description: Spring's arrival across Africa reveals the life-giving gifts in the continent's plant and animal life; a close-up look at African animal families raising their young and their emergence into the wider world.

Subject: Two and a Half Men - Pilot
Description: Charlie's life turns upside down when his brother and nephew move in; at the supermarket, Charlie discovers he can use 10-year-old Jake to attract women.

Subject: Two and a Half Men - Big Flappy Bastards
Description: Charlie doesn't know how to discipline Jake for luring a flock of sea gulls into his room; Judith surprises Alan when he goes home to fix her sprinklers.

Subject: Two and a Half Men - Go East on Sunset Until You Reach the Gates of Hell
Description: One drink too many emboldens the brothers to stand up to their controlling mother.

Subject: Two and a Half Men - If I Can't Write My Chocolate Song, I'm Going to Take a Nap
Description: Alan offers to help get Charlie's cleaning lady back if Charlie can get Jake ready to go out to dinner with his mother.

Subject: Two and a Half Men - The Last Thing You Want Is to Wind Up With a Hump
Description: Charlie and Alan meet several women while attending one of Jake's soccer games.

Subject: Two and a Half Men - Did You Check With the Captain of the Flying Monkeys?
Description: After Evelyn's new beau dumps her, Charlie and Alan enlist Jake to cheer her up; Charlie teaches Jake a lesson about screening telephone calls from women.

Subject: Two and a Half Men - If They Do Go Either Way, They're Usually Fake
Description: For a school assignment, Jake draws the tattooed buttocks of Charlie's female friend.

Subject: Two and a Half Men - Twenty-Five Little Pre-Pubers Without a Snoot-Ful
Description: Charlie realizes Jake's classmates know the words to all of his jingles, and he is inspired to craft the fourth-grade musical with unusual results.

Subject: A Bronx Tale (1993)
Description: The son (Lillo Brancato) of an honest bus driver (Robert De Niro) looks up to a local mob boss (Chazz Palminteri) amid racial tension in 1960s New York.

Subject: A Few Good Men (1992)
Description: Navy lawyers defend two Marines accused of killing a private at the naval station at Guantánamo Bay, Cuba.

Subject: Road House (1989)
Description: Hired to tame a rowdy Missouri bar, a Ph.D. bouncer (Patrick Swayze) romances a doctor (Kelly Lynch) and tames the whole town.

Subject: Cocktail (1988)
Description: The hottest bartender (Tom Cruise) in Manhattan leaves his partner (Bryan Brown), goes to Jamaica and falls for a nice girl (Elisabeth Shue).

Subject: Law & Order - Everybody's Favorite Bagman
Description: A mugging investigation leads to city corruption involving a councilman and the parking violations bureau.

Subject: Law & Order - Prescription for Death
Description: The emergency-room death of a young woman may lead to a negligence charge for the hospital's chief of medicine.

Subject: Law & Order - Doped
Description: A suspicious nasal spray is found at the site of a deadly car crash; after the medical examiner realizes the spray could easily disorient its user, the detectives become suspicious of the victim's boss.

Subject: Law & Order - Boy Gone Astray
Description: When a wealthy young woman is found dead in her apartment, a few signs lead detectives to believe the killer was not after her wealth; a security video exposes two potential suspects, leading the detectives to a drug cartel.

Subject: Law & Order - Human Flesh Search Engine
Description: The revelation of a murder victim's true identity leads detectives to a suspicious web site with threatening posts.

Subject: Law & Order - Dignity
Description: When a doctor is murdered, the detectives investigate an abortion protester; Van Buren receives more bad news; Connie faces a moral dilemma.

Subject: Law & Order - Reality Bites
Description: While searching for the murderer of the adoptive mother of 10 disabled children, detectives uncover a reality-show offer and an affair.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl I: Kansas City Chiefs vs. Green Bay Packers
Description: The Packers defeat the Chiefs 35-10 in the first Super Bowl in NFL history. From Jan. 15, 1967 at Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum in Los Angeles.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl II: Green Bay Packers vs. Oakland Raiders
Description: Legendary Packers head coach Vince Lombardi wins his second Super Bowl in as many years defeating the Oakland Raiders 33-14. From Jan. 14, 1968 at the Orange Bowl in Miami.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl III: New York Jets vs. Baltimore Colts
Description: The New York Jets stun the Baltimore Colts defeating them by a score of 16-7. The Jets were 16-point underdogs, but quarterback Joe Namath boldly guaranteed his team would win the game. From Jan. 12, 1969 at Orange Bowl in Miami.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl IV: Kansas City Chiefs vs. Minnesota Vikings
Description: The last AFL-NFL game before the merger. The Chiefs dominate the Vikings giving them their first Super Bowl title. From Jan. 11, 1970 at Tulane Stadium in New Orleans.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl V: Baltimore Colts vs. Dallas Cowboys
Description: The Colts beat the Cowboys 16-13 on rookie kicker Jim O'Brien's 32-yard field goal in the final five seconds. Chuck Howley earns the game's MVP award. From Jan. 17, 1971 at Orange Bowl in Miami.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl VI: Dallas Cowboys vs. Miami Dolphins
Description: The Cowboys rush for a record 252 yards and their defense limited the Dolphins to a low of 185 yards while not permitting a touchdown for the first time in Super Bowl history. From Jan. 16, 1972 at Tulane Stadium in New Orleans.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl VII: Miami Dolphins vs. Washington Redskins
Description: The Dolphins beat the Washington Redskins 14-7 to become the only team in NFL history to complete a perfect season (17-0). From Jan. 14, 1973 at Los Angeles Memorial Coliseum in Los Angeles.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl VIII: Miami Dolphins vs. Minnestoa Vikings
Description: The Dolphins defeat the Vikings by a score of 24-7 to become the second team in NFL history to win back-to-back Super Bowls. From Jan. 13, 1974 at Rice Stadium in Houston.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl IX: Pittsburgh Steelers vs. Minnesota Vikings
Description: Steelers running back Franco Harris rushes for 158 yards and a touchdown earning Super Bowl MVP honors as the Steelers beat the Vikings 16-6. From Jan. 12, 1975 at Tulane Stadium in New Orleans.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl X: Pittsburgh Steelers vs. Dallas Cowboys
Description: Lynn Swann is named Super Bowl MVP as the Steelers win their second straight NFL Championship. From Jan. 18, 1976 at Orange Bowl in Miami.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XI: Oakland Raiders vs. Minnesota Vikings
Description: Under head coach John Madden, the Raiders win their first Super Bowl in franchise history by defeating the Vikings 32-14. From Jan. 9, 1977 at Rose Bowl in Pasadena, Calif.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XII: Dallas Cowboys vs. Denver Broncos
Description: Roger Staubach and the ``Doomsday Defense`` of Dallas defeat the Denver Broncos and win their fourth Super Bowl. This Super Bowl was the first one to be played in a domed stadium. From Louisiana Superdome in New Orleans.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XIII: Pittsburgh Steelers vs. Dallas Cowboys
Description: Steelers become first team in NFL history to win three Super Bowls, defeating the Cowboys 35-31. Terry Bradshaw is the MVP after passing for record 4 touchdowns and 318 yards.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XIV: Pittsburgh Steelers vs. Los Angeles Rams
Description: The Steelers defeat the Los Angeles Rams 31-19 to become the first team in NFL history to win four Super Bowls. From Jan. 20, 1980 at Rose Bowl in Pasadena, Calif.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XV: Oakland Raiders vs. Philadelphia Eagles
Description: The Raiders are the first wild card team to win a Super Bowl by defeating the Eagles 27-10. From Jan. 25, 1981 at Louisiana Superdome in New Orleans.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XVI: San Francisco 49ers vs. Cincinnati Bengals
Description: San Francisco jumps ahead to a 20-point halftime lead and doesn't let go for the 26-21 win over the Bengals. From Jan. 24, 1982 at Pontiac Silverdome in Pontiac, Mich.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XVII: Miami Dolphins vs. Washington Redskins
Description: Redskins fullback John Riggins ran for 166 yards on 38 carries to spark the Redskins 27-17 victory over the Dolphins. From Jan. 30, 1983 at Rose Bowl in Pasadena, Calif.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XVIII: Los Angeles Raiders vs. Washington Redskins
Description: The Raiders defeat the Redskins 38-9 behind running back Marcus Allen's Super Bowl record 191 rushing yards. From Jan. 22, 1984 at Tampa Stadium in Tampa, Fla.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XIX: San Francisco 49ers vs. Miami Dolphins
Description: Joe Montana passes for 331 yards and 3 touchdowns leading San Francisco to a 38-16 victory over the Dolphins. From Jan. 20, 1985 at Stanford Stadium in Stanford, Calif.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XX: Chicago Bears vs. New England Patriots
Description: The Bears overpower the Patriots 46-10 winning the franchise's first NFL title since 1963. From Jan. 26, 1986 at Louisiana Superdome in New Orleans.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXI: New York Giants vs. Denver Broncos
Description: The Giants score 30 second-half points under quarterback Phil Simms on their way to a 39-20 victory over the Broncos. From Jan. 25, 1987 at Rose Bowl in Pasadena, Calif.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXII: Denver Broncos vs. Washington Redskins
Description: Redskins erupt in the second quarter, scoring 35 points, and defeat the Broncos 42-10. Doug Williams is the first African-American quarterback to win the Super Bowl and earn MVP honors. From Jan. 31, 1988 at Jack Murphy Stadium in San Diego.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXIII: San Francisco 49ers vs. Cincinnati Bengals
Description: Trailing late in the fourth quarter Joe Montana leads the 49ers on a legendary drive that culminates with a 10-yard touchdown pass to John Taylor and the 49ers third Super Bowl win of the decade. From Jan. 22, 1989 at Joe Robbie Stadium in Miami.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXIV: San Francisco 49ers vs. Denver Broncos
Description: The San Francisco 49ers, led by MVP Joe Montana, become the second team in NFL history to win four Super Bowls with a convincing 55-10 victory. From Jan. 28, 1990 at Louisiana Superdome in New Orleans.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXV: New York Giants vs. Buffalo Bills
Description: The Giants win their second Super Bowl in five years by defeating the Bills 20-19. Scott Norwood's 47-yard field goal sails wide right with no time left in the game to preserve the Giants victory. From Jan. 27, 1991 at Tampa Stadium in Tampa, Fla.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXVI: Buffalo Bills vs. Washington Redskins
Description: Redskins quarterback Mark Rypien passes for 292 yards and two touchdowns earning game MVP honors as the Redskins defeat the Buffalo Bills 37-24. From Jan. 26, 1992 at Hubert H. Humphrey Metrodome in Minneapolis, Minn.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXVII: Dallas Cowboys vs. Buffalo Bills
Description: Cowboys quarterback Troy Aikman earns MVP honors by passing for 273 yards and 4 touchdowns leading Dallas to a 52-17 victory over the Buffalo Bills. From Jan. 31, 1993 at Rose Bowl in Pasadena, Calif.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXVIII: Buffalo Bills vs. Dallas Cowboys
Description: Running back Emmitt Smith rushes for 132 yards and 2 touchdowns earning Super Bowl MVP honors as the Cowboys defeat the Bills 30-13 to win their second consecutive NFL title. From Jan. 30, 1994 at Georgia Dome in Atlanta.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXIX: San Francisco 49ers vs. San Diego Chargers
Description: Quarterback Steve Young passes for a record 6 touchdowns leading the 49ers to a 49-26 victory over the Chargers. From Jan. 29, 1995 at Joe Robbie Stadium in Miami.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXX: Dallas Cowboys vs. Pittsburgh Steelers
Description: Dallas cornerback Larry Brown intercepts 2 passes that lead to 14 second-half points as the Cowboys record their third Super Bowl victory in four years defeating the Steelers 27-17. From Jan. 28, 1996 at Sun Devil Stadium in Tempe, Ariz.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXXI: Green Bay Packers vs. New England Patriots
Description: Desmond Howard returns a kickoff 99 yards and quarterback Brett Favre passes for 2 touchdowns as the Packers defeat the Patriots 35-21 to win their first Super Bowl in twenty-nine years. From Jan. 26, 1997 at Louisiana Superdome in New Orleans.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXXII: Green Bay Packers vs. Denver Broncos
Description: Terrell Davis rushes for 157 yards and 3 touchdowns to lead the Broncos to a 31-24 victory over the Packers and the franchise's first NFL championship. From Jan. 25, 1998 at Qualcomm Stadium in San Diego.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXXIII: Atlanta Falcons vs. Denver Broncos
Description: In his last game, Broncos' quarterback John Elway passes for 336 yards and runs for a touchdown to earn MVP honors as the Broncos defeated the Falcons 34-19. From Jan. 31, 1999 at Pro Player Stadium in Miami.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXXIV: St. Louis Rams vs. Tennessee Titans
Description: The St. Louis Rams defeat the Tennessee Titans 23-16 after linebacker Mike Jones makes a game saving tackle on the 1-yard line as time expires preserving the Rams first-ever Super Bowl Championship. From Jan. 30, 2000 at Georgia Dome in Atlanta.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXXV: Baltimore Ravens vs. New York Giants
Description: Baltimore Ravens dominate the New York Giants, forcing 5 turnovers and recording 4 sacks en route to the franchise's first Super Bowl victory. From Jan. 28, 2001 at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Fla.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXXVI: New England Patriots vs. St. Louis Rams
Description: Adam Vinatieri's 48-yard field goal as time expires gives the Patriots their first Super Bowl title in franchise history defeating the Rams 20-17. From Feb. 3, 2002 at Louisiana Superdome in New Orleans.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXXVII: Oakland Raiders vs. Tampa Bay Buccaneers
Description: Behind Warren Sapp and Derrick Brooks, the Buccaneers' defense intercept five passes en route to the franchise's first Super Bowl victory 48-21 over the Raiders. From Jan. 26, 2003 at Qualcomm Stadium in San Diego.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXXVIII: Carolina Panthers vs. New England Patriots
Description: Tom Brady passes for 354 yards and three touchdowns as the Patriots defeat the Panthers 32-29. Adam Vinatieri kicks a 41-yard field goal with four seconds left to secure the victory for New England. From Feb. 1, 2004 at Reliant Stadium in Houston.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XXXIX: New England Patriots vs. Philadelphia Eagles
Description: Tom Brady passes for 236 yards and two touchdowns as he leads the Patriots to their third Super Bowl championship in four years over Donovan McNabb and the Philadelphia Eagles. From Feb. 6, 2005 at Alltel Stadium in Jacksonville, Fla.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XL: Pittsburgh Steelers vs. Seattle Seahawks
Description: Hines Ward propels the Steelers to victory over the Seahawks 21-10 and Pittsburgh becomes the third team in NFL history to have won five Super Bowls. From Feb. 5, 2006 at Ford Field in Detroit.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XLI: Indianapolis Colts vs. Chicago Bears
Description: Peyton Manning leads the Indianapolis Colts past the Chicago Bears 29-17. Lovie Smith and Tony Dungy both become the first African-American head coaches to coach in the Super Bowl. From Feb. 4, 2007 at Dolphin Stadium in Miami.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XLII: New England Patriots vs. New York Giants
Description: Eli Manning and the Giants shock the Patriots with one of the biggest upsets in Super Bowl history to end the Patriots' quest for a perfect season. From Feb. 3, 2008 at University of Phoenix Stadium in Glendale, Ariz.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XLIII: Arizona Cardinals vs. Pittsburgh Steelers
Description: Ben Roethlisberger leads the Steelers to a dramatic come-from-behind victory over the Cardinals 27-23 in a wild back-and-forth game that set 12 Super Bowl records. From Feb. 1, 2009 at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Fla.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XLIV: New Orleans Saints vs. Indianapolis Colts
Description: Drew Brees and the New Orleans Saints cap off a magical season by beating Peyton Manning and the Indianapolis Colts for their first Super Bowl win. From Feb. 7, 2010 at Sun Life Stadium in Miami.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XLV: Green Bay Packers vs. Pittsburgh Steelers
Description: Quarterback Aaron Rodgers lead the Green Bay Packers to the 31-25 victory over the Pittsburgh Steelers and earns MVP honors. From Feb. 6, 2011 at Cowboys Stadium in Arlington, Texas.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XLVI: New England Patriots vs. New York Giants
Description: In a rematch of Super Bowl XLII, the Giants defeat the Patriots, 21-17. Eli Manning posts an MVP-worthy performance. From Feb. 5, 2012 at Lucas Oil Stadium in Indianapolis.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XLVII: Baltimore Ravens vs. San Francisco 49ers
Description: The Baltimore Ravens defeat the San Francisco 49ers in a 34-31 victory. John and Jim Harbaugh become the first brothers in Super Bowl history to coach against each other. From Feb. 3, 2013 at Mercedes-Benz Superdome in New Orleans.

Subject: Super Bowl Highlights - Super Bowl XLVIII: Seattle Seahawks vs. Denver Broncos
Description: The Seattle Seahawks and their top ranked defense help deliver the first Super Bowl in franchise history by punishing Peyton Manning and the Denver Broncos 43-8. From Feb. 2, 2014 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, N.J.

Subject: College Football - SEC Championship: Georgia vs. Alabama
Description: The No. 9 Crimson Tide (10-2) and No. 3 Bulldogs (11-1) meet in the SEC championship game for the third time in five years. Alabama is 4-0 against Georgia in SEC title contests. The Bulldogs yielded a total of 22 points in the last three outings.

Subject: College Football - LSU at Vanderbilt
Description: The No. 10 Tigers (5-1, 2-1) visit the No. 17 Commodores (5-1, 1-1) for an SEC showdown. LSU has won 10 straight meetings since Vanderbilt prevailed in 1990. The Tigers are fifth nationally in fewest points allowed at 11.8 points per contest.
